title: vachellia leucophloea var. leucophloea
intro: Vachellia leucophloea var. leucophloea, also known as the golden neem or gum arabic tree, is a deciduous tree native to the Indian subcontinent. It is a medicinal plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in Ayurveda, with properties that include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Vachellia leucophloea var. leucophloea include reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems, while its herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications. Additionally, the tree's gum resin is a key ingredient in the production of gum arabic, a natural adhesive and emulsifier used in various industries.
